169452.explanation: A municipality cannot function without cooperation. For major challenges that cross municipal borders but directly affect our own residents, cooperation is important. Examples include the station area, sheltering target groups, the environmental service, and procurement of services.

169454.explanation: From our Christian background, Sunday rest is important, which does not include active shopping.

169456.explanation: CDA Hardenberg is fundamentally not opposed to distribution centers, provided they fit within environmental or zoning plans and create employment opportunities for our municipality.

169457.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes that people who come to our country but have no chance of settling here should return to their country of origin as soon as possible.

169458.explanation: We believe a level playing field for housing seekers is important. We find it important that residents of our municipality who want to stay in their own community are given priority.

169459.explanation: CDA does not want to maintain the current arrangement used in Hardenberg. Shelter in this form is no longer desirable; we opt for small-scale shelter according to the Hardenbergs model: consideration for the environment and small, suitable locations for families only.

169461.explanation: CDA Hardenberg wants to make new forms of housing in rural areas easier, for example in or near vacant agricultural buildings or by allowing more temporary homes.

169462.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes more should be invested in health prevention: encouraging people to make healthy choices, both physically and mentally. Also encouraging people to exercise and play sports, especially those for whom financial barriers make it difficult to get active.

169463.explanation: Although CDA Hardenberg believes children should learn to swim, we currently find school swimming unfeasible. We have too few swimming pools, transport options, and space, making it impossible to organize this well and safely.

169464.explanation: CDA Hardenberg supports a multi-year maintenance plan for all schools, with occasional review of needs where relevant.

169465.explanation: CDA Hardenberg finds it important that our regional language is properly passed on to future generations, with attention to the language in schools being a valuable addition to the curriculum.

169466.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes we should act preventively rather than repressively. More attention to how we treat each other.

169467.explanation: CDA Hardenberg does not believe in the tolerance policy on soft drugs; the tolerance policy promotes crime, nuisance, and health problems.

169468.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes research should be done into the support for possible camera surveillance in shopping centers of the various communities.

169470.explanation: CDA Hardenberg wants sufficient housing for all ages and target groups. In addition to social rental homes, CDA wants to focus on mid-rent homes for people who earn too much for social rent but for whom buying is (still) not feasible.

169471.explanation: Living in a holiday park is generally not allowed. CDA Hardenberg advocates clear rules and believes that people who are forced to live in a recreational home should not live in uncertainty. CDA pleads for protection of residents who have lived there for years.

169472.explanation: CDA Hardenberg is careful with our outdoor spaces. We are not against new construction outside villages but believe infill should always precede expansion.

169473.explanation: CDA Hardenberg finds participation of all our residents very important, of course taking into account the (in)abilities of our residents. CDA Hardenberg believes that day activities bring more than just a compensation. Participating in society is very important.

169474.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes it is very important that our shopping centers remain easily accessible. Safe and good access roads and sufficient parking facilities are very important.

169476.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes participation in the living environment should be possible. We do not believe there always has to be compensation for this.

169477.explanation: CDA Hardenberg attaches great value to good administrators. Preferably from our own municipality.

169478.explanation: The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. However, it is important to involve citizens in different ways on important topics.

169480.explanation: CDA Hardenberg considers the use of AI indispensable in our society. However, we advocate for personal contact when it is necessary or preferred.

169481.explanation: CDA Hardenberg supports further research into the renovation or rebuilding of theater de Voorveghter.

169482.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes that sports and culture should be as accessible as possible. In our view, this does not necessarily require free admission.

169483.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes that public outdoor spaces should be designed in such a way that social interaction and physical activity naturally occur and are accessible to everyone.

169484.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes we should focus on rich identities within education, so that schools retain space for their own identity and values with respect for differences.

169485.explanation: We believe that education must be suitable and inclusive for all our residents.

169486.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes that a terrace policy can provide clear guidelines for terraces. Within the framework, entrepreneurs can make their own choices.

169488.explanation: With the tourist tax, outsiders contribute to the maintenance of our facilities. These taxes allow us to keep other charges low.

169490.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes we should not house more labor migrants than the local businesses in our municipality need. We must always prevent exploitation of people.

169491.explanation: CDA believes that residents should be motivated but should not receive financial compensation.

169492.explanation: Waste processing costs money, which we pay through the waste collection levy. CDA wants to keep these levies as low as possible.

169493.explanation: CDA is neither for nor against. Such plans should always be studied and weighed against the environment.

169494.explanation: CDA Hardenberg wants to increase livability by making the city center more attractive, partly by researching a city beach and/or event area.

169495.explanation: CDA Hardenberg understands the (financial) consideration to collect waste every 6 weeks but believes that in every community there should be the option to bring residual waste to a collection point.

169497.explanation: We believe we should focus on prevention and safety around schools.

169499.explanation: CDA Hardenberg considers prevention and a public awareness campaign important to increase awareness.

169502.explanation: We consider it important that rental homes are maintained.

169503.explanation: Municipalities must, like other employers, provide appropriate compensation depending on education and job duties.

169505.explanation: CDA Hardenberg considers this the personal responsibility of pet owners. We value animal welfare but believe this does not automatically mean the municipality must cover these costs.

169506.explanation: CDA values equal opportunities for people with a distance to the labor market, inclusion, and diversity. CDA is cautious about strict quotas.

169507.explanation: CDA Hardenberg agrees with researching the feasibility and added value of a bypass.

169509.explanation: CDA Hardenberg wants to first invest in good cycling facilities on the edges of Hardenberg’s center (covered parking, charging points, and a cycling ring road) before discussing cycling in the city center.

169510.explanation: CDA Hardenberg wants to discourage residents from using bicycles in the city center shopping area. We want to invest in good cycling facilities such as parking, charging points, and a cycling ring road.

169511.explanation: Visits to the city center should be as accessible as possible.

169512.explanation: CDA Hardenberg wants to ensure safe road design near primary schools with measures such as lower speeds, safe crossings, and clear school zones so children can get to school safely.

169513.explanation: CDA Hardenberg thinks it is very important that young people can meet each other in a safe place.

169514.explanation: CDA wants to support such an initiative. But we have reservations about the term 'must'.

169516.explanation: CDA Hardenberg believes a contribution can be requested for light support such as homework guidance. CDA believes parents should not pay for indicated care: no financial barriers in youth care.

169452.explanation: The municipality must gain more control over that cooperation.

169454.explanation: We leave the choice up to the entrepreneur.

169456.explanation: When it does not fit within the regional function of the municipality.

169457.explanation: Too many problems with ‘safe countries of origin’.

169461.explanation: They must comply with the general conditions, but not to the detriment of transit farmers.

169464.explanation: This already happens through the Education Housing Plan.

169467.explanation: The municipality should be cautious with facilities that make drug use more accessible.

169468.explanation: First adjusting the regulations; currently almost nothing can be done with the recordings.

169470.explanation: In accordance with national agreements and guidelines.

169471.explanation: There is a high chance that this will lead to undermining or undesirable excesses.

169472.explanation: The facilities are located in the villages.

169473.explanation: If they are capable of doing so.

169474.explanation: Further limiting this accessibility should only happen in good consultation with the shopkeepers.

169476.explanation: To involve residents in their living environment.

169477.explanation: Our experience with aldermen from outside is excellent!

169478.explanation: We support broad advisory input, but not through lottery selection.

169480.explanation: OpKoers.nu prefers personal service. OpKoers.nu believes that our citizens should be able to ask their questions personally and receive personal answers.

169481.explanation: New builds or renovations that suit Hardenberg.

169482.explanation: However, attention must be paid to residents who cannot afford it; under certain circumstances, they can rely on a form of assistance to participate.

169483.explanation: Where it appears necessary and desired through good consultation with our residents.

169484.explanation: Provided that first there is a thorough and critical look at smart and financially responsible options, such as good cooperation.

169485.explanation: Parents are mostly not capable of doing that.

169486.explanation: Having no rules at all causes chaos.

169490.explanation: This is a task of the national government; the municipality must still pay attention to this subject.

169492.explanation: For private individuals, not for commercial companies.

169494.explanation: But that also applies to other centers in our municipality.

169495.explanation: Unless it turns out that the current arrangement leads to problems.

169497.explanation: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) have clear roles and tasks, contributing to livability and welfare. Investigative powers under the Opium Act are reserved for the police and the public prosecutor's office.

169499.explanation: This should primarily be addressed well at the national level, as this problem exceeds the municipality.

169502.explanation: It is important to realize that the municipality has no authority over this.

169503.explanation: Interns often work hard, and good, satisfied interns tend to ‘stick around,’ helping the municipality to find good employees. However, it is an internship position, not a job, so more an extension of school or training.

169505.explanation: There are arrangements for this; the municipality should mainly align with the existing regulations and possibilities.

169506.explanation: One must have the right knowledge and competences.

169507.explanation: The municipality must invest money in preparing it.

169509.explanation: In parts of the shopping center and especially in good consultation with our residents and entrepreneurs.

169513.explanation: If there are youth, for and by the youth.

169514.explanation: Although it is a challenge to increasingly involve our young people in politics, we do not prefer to establish a child mayor.

169516.explanation: Provided they are capable of doing so; if that is not the case, safety nets are needed in the interest of our children.

169454.explanation: The Christian Union values a good balance between work, care, and rest. From our conviction, we cherish Sunday as a moment of rest. God did not give us six days to work and a seventh to enjoy rest, church services, reflection, and family for nothing.

169456.explanation: We do not want large landscape-disfiguring buildings that do not contribute to our local economy.

169457.explanation: A fair reception policy also requires a fair deportation policy; this is determined nationally.

169458.explanation: We ensure there is a sufficient housing supply for everyone looking for a home.

169459.explanation: We support small-scale shelters spread across residential areas so newcomers can actively participate and integrate into our local community.

169461.explanation: For this, we already have a good scheme in our municipality called 'Erven met kwaliteit' (Inherited with Quality).

169465.explanation: We want to promote the dialect, but believe that education should be kept separate from politics.

169466.explanation: The quantity must be maintained and safety is not only about physical safety, but also digital and social safety.

169470.explanation: We do, however, follow the demand present in the market.

169471.explanation: We believe holiday parks are for recreation and not for permanent residence.

169472.explanation: Prioritizing within village boundaries always comes first, but given the current housing shortage, the situation calls for 'adding a street here and there when necessary.'

169473.explanation: This encourages contributing to society and helps people return to paid work.

169477.explanation: Quality is more important than quantity, but we do believe there must be a connection with our municipality.

169480.explanation: We find a personal approach important.

169481.explanation: An investigation is underway into various possibilities. We want to bring the theater up to the standards of this time.

169482.explanation: Every young person should have the opportunity to come into contact with sports and culture.

169484.explanation: We find it important that facilities, including schools, in small communities remain open.

169486.explanation: We have regulations in place, especially closely monitoring safety.

169488.explanation: We value quality over quantity, with tourist tax revenue flowing back into improving the environment, including safe cycling connections, walking paths, and nature restoration.

169492.explanation: For this, we want to set a maximum number of kilos per household.

169495.explanation: We do believe young families and elderly should be provided a solution for diapers and incontinence materials.

169497.explanation: We strive for a drug-free society.

169506.explanation: We support hiring people with disabilities, but we already have existing schemes for this, which are not fixed to 25 employees.

169509.explanation: Only during shopping hours and the bicycle parking facilities must be properly maintained.

169511.explanation: We want to encourage alternative transportation.

169516.explanation: This is part of the municipality's care responsibilities.

169452.explanation: Some matters can be arranged more effectively in cooperation with other municipalities, for example (specialist) mental health care. However, the Council should be more involved and have better oversight of collaborations.

169454.explanation: If shops want to open on Sundays, we want to make that possible.

169456.explanation: The municipality must make space for businesses that truly contribute to our community: local entrepreneurs and companies with a social role in the neighborhood or village. Distribution centers with few jobs and high energy consumption do not fit here.

169457.explanation: If an asylum application is rejected and there is no longer any appeal/objection possible, people must return. However, it cannot be that these people end up homeless wandering until their return to their country of origin. Therefore, we must provide special regional shelter locations.

169458.explanation: Various groups get priority for rental housing, including recognized refugees, as it is undesirable for people with a residence status to remain in an asylum seekers' center and be limited in their integration.

169459.explanation: Hardenberg must fulfill its moral duty and offer a safe place for people fleeing war and violence. There does not need to be a new asylum seekers' center the size of AZC Jachthuisweg, but we must provide shelter for the number indicated by the dispersion law. That is about 400 refugees.

169461.explanation: If it fits within the environment, it must be possible.

169462.explanation: It cannot be that children do not participate in sports because they cannot afford it. We also want to make this possible not only for children but also for adults. This keeps our municipality healthy.

169463.explanation: Knowing how to swim is a matter of life and death. By reintroducing school swimming lessons, we ensure all children master the basics.

169464.explanation: School buildings with healthy clean air are important. There are buildings that still need attention. We allocate funds for that.

169465.explanation: Protecting and strengthening the regional language is important. We want to do this not only through schools but also with various cultural partners such as museums, libraries, and historical associations.

169466.explanation: There has been a significant expansion of the enforcement team recently. We maintain staffing levels but currently see no reason for further expansion.

169467.explanation: If an entrepreneur complies with the rules and conditions, the establishment of a coffeeshop should be possible.

169468.explanation: When privacy is guaranteed, we make that possible in specific cases. We especially focus on locations where street intimidation occurs.

169470.explanation: Therefore, a concrete plan is necessary to achieve 30% social housing within ten years. This can only be done by building significantly more social rental homes each year.

169471.explanation: Holiday homes are often not suitable for permanent residence. Only if a holiday park as a whole is suitable for permanent residence do we allow it.

169472.explanation: We do not want to exclude that. When there are opportunities for a project that fits within the environment, we look at it positively. The housing crisis means we should not exclude too much. Of course, building within village/city boundaries is preferred.

169473.explanation: Socially meaningful work (such as volunteer work) prevents isolation, strengthens self-esteem, and keeps people active as part of society. We will determine together with the resident which type of involvement suits them best.

169474.explanation: There are good parking facilities on the edges of the center. We will adjust the roads around the center to make movement safer for cyclists and pedestrians. Especially within the built-up area, we mainly provide space for cyclists and pedestrians.

169476.explanation: We want to enable that with clear agreements about the method of maintenance.

169477.explanation: We need aldermen who govern Hardenberg well. Whether they have lived in the municipality for the past 10 years says nothing about the suitability of a leader.

169478.explanation: With clear agreements in advance about how the council should handle these recommendations.

169480.explanation: The quality of information must be guaranteed. At present, using a chatbot carries too high a risk of incorrect information.

169481.explanation: The theater must be made future-proof. This can be through renovation or new construction. The necessary information to make that choice is currently missing.

169482.explanation: This way we make sports and culture more accessible and ensure a healthy and vibrant municipality where finances are not an obstacle to participation.

169483.explanation: People want to participate in individual sports more in their own time. We facilitate that and promote a healthy municipality.

169484.explanation: People must be able to choose a suitable school. An accessible public school must also be available in all population centers.

169485.explanation: Language is the foundation for participation in our municipality. If there is a delay in this area, we want to address it. Children will benefit from this for the rest of their lives.

169486.explanation: With clear conditions, we want to make that possible. Public spaces must remain well accessible, also for people in wheelchairs for example.

169488.explanation: Tourists come here for the green environment but also impact nature. Therefore, we find it obvious to use the tourist tax for the preservation of our nature.

169490.explanation: In collaboration with organizations advocating for labor migrants and trade unions.

169491.explanation: We want to encourage the greening of gardens. This can be through financial contributions but also with free garden plants.

169492.explanation: We will maintain the current waste tariffs. Waste processing is not free; it is therefore justified that dumping is not free. Otherwise, the entire municipality would bear the costs.

169493.explanation: New large parks no longer fit in the environment and would cause too much nuisance and pressure on nature. In some cases, expansion is possible, but this must be assessed case by case.

169494.explanation: We would rather spend that money on other matters. We find this unnecessary especially since Oldemeijer already has this function. Furthermore, it will impact the nature around the Vecht, which is already under pressure from recreational use.

169495.explanation: It has proven that once every six weeks for residual waste causes no problems. Especially with good waste separation, there is no reason for this and it would only lead to higher costs for the resident.

169497.explanation: Safety around schools has fortunately already received much attention, also from the police and the schools themselves. Furthermore, combating drug trafficking is not a task for municipal enforcement officers but for the police.

169499.explanation: By targeted information, we increase the digital resilience of especially vulnerable residents against online fraud and abuse.

169502.explanation: There is a great shortage of social rental housing. Sale by social housing corporations is not appropriate now.

169503.explanation: Interns must receive a fair compensation of €750 per month for a full-time internship.

169505.explanation: There must be provisions for unforeseen matters of this kind and applications should be processed swiftly.

169506.explanation: The municipality must be an inclusive employer and also do its part to offer people with distance to the labor market a place.

169507.explanation: This would cost a huge sum of money that we would rather use for other matters. Moreover, it will have a major impact on nature and animals.

169509.explanation: The center has become busier. Cyclists among shoppers pose a danger. Therefore, we first ensure good bike parking at the start of the shopping area and then introduce a cycling ban during shopping hours.

169510.explanation: This way, we ensure that less cycling occurs in the shopping area and more people can come by bike instead of by car.

169511.explanation: We see no reason for this. We want to encourage bicycle use and find it justified that parking must be paid for, but we are not in favor of an increase.

169512.explanation: Not only at schools, but also in the centers of various population centers improvements can be made.

169513.explanation: Every population center must have a meeting place. In smaller centers, this can be arranged by making a community center or another social building available to youth for an evening.

169514.explanation: We have the youth advisory council. They fulfill an important representative role for the youth. A child mayor would add little value. The youth advisory council can handle such tasks perfectly themselves.

169516.explanation: This makes it inaccessible for some children if their parents have a lower income.

169452.explanation: An example is student transport, which Hardenberg has arranged better than neighboring municipalities.

169454.explanation: Entrepreneurs have the freedom and space to make their own choices about the opening of their shop.

169456.explanation: Industrial space is scarce and must be made available to entrepreneurs who provide employment to Hardenberg residents: no large warehouses where no one works.

169457.explanation: Hardenberg has taken its responsibility. As far as the VVD is concerned, that is enough for now and it's the other municipalities' turn.

169458.explanation: Our residents get priority over status holders.

169459.explanation: Hardenberg has taken its responsibility in recent decades, while other municipalities have shirked theirs. It is their turn first now.

169462.explanation: The safety net already exists, and this is paid for from the youth fund.

169463.explanation: We consider sports and exercise important; we want to see children learn to swim at a young age.

169464.explanation: Keeping school buildings up to date and making them sustainable is important to us.

169465.explanation: We consider this unnecessary and not a municipal task.

169466.explanation: Safety is an important priority for the VVD.

169467.explanation: One coffeeshop is sufficient in Hardenberg.

169468.explanation: The past has shown that this is necessary according to the VVD.

169470.explanation: Build according to demand and do not fix it at a percentage.

169471.explanation: The municipality must maintain control. No uncontrolled growth of residential addresses at holiday parks.

169472.explanation: It has become clear that it is sometimes difficult within existing boundaries. This means the municipality could also look beyond the village borders, but still keep control itself.

169473.explanation: According to the VVD, everyone must participate and contribute to society.

169474.explanation: Infrastructure is important for our entrepreneurs and residents.

169476.explanation: We welcome everything that can be done better and more cost-effectively.

169477.explanation: The quality of an alderman is more important than his place of residence.

169478.explanation: The council is the people's representative and is expected to make expert judgments and decisions.

169480.explanation: We should embrace new technologies as much as possible in a secured manner.

169481.explanation: The theater must be functionally adapted. No 50 million for a new building!

169484.explanation: Diverse education is important to us so that everyone can make their own choices.

169485.explanation: Children must be given the best possible start!

169486.explanation: We are for fewer rules and more space to undertake business.

169488.explanation: Tourist tax must be spent on things tourists find important to help recreation entrepreneurs.

169490.explanation: Every form of exploitation must be countered.

169491.explanation: People should be free to do what they want in their own garden: the municipality must stay out!

169493.explanation: The municipality should give recreation entrepreneurs space to conduct business!

169494.explanation: There is already a great beach at the Oldemeijer.

169495.explanation: Research has shown that the vast majority of people are satisfied with the current frequency!

169497.explanation: For example, at secondary education in Hardenberg and Dedemsvaart.

169499.explanation: We consider contributing to making our residents digitally more resilient important. Recently, for example, a judge ruled that online bullying is also punishable.

169502.explanation: Moreover: sitting tenants are allowed to buy the rented house. This gives Vechtdal Wonen the possibility to continuously build more new houses!

169503.explanation: The VVD finds this more than enough!

169506.explanation: The municipality must hire employees who are qualified for the job.

169507.explanation: Mobility and accessibility are of great importance!

169509.explanation: Accessibility is very important to us and our entrepreneurs.

169511.explanation: Revenue must cover the costs to ensure that the city center remains lively.

169512.explanation: Of course, children must be able to cross safely near schools.

169513.explanation: We gladly support initiatives from local communities.

169514.explanation: It sounds nice, but adds little in practice.

169516.explanation: Every child has the right to a good start!

169452.explanation: Regional cooperation is becoming increasingly important to properly perform tasks. However, we must ensure that the activities and goals within these collaborations are transparent and easily auditable.

169454.explanation: We leave this as much as possible to the entrepreneurs.

169456.explanation: Large distribution centers disproportionately put pressure on the electricity grid and infrastructure and create few jobs.

169457.explanation: In cases of illegal residence, the government must comply with national laws and regulations. Compassion from society has our approval.

169458.explanation: Here too, we are bound by national laws and regulations. In addition, these people have often been in the Netherlands so long that if they had been on a waiting list from the start, they would have had their turn for a house. It will also contribute to better and faster integration.

169459.explanation: Our municipality must provide small-scale shelter locations in balance with the environment. We want to support and facilitate local initiatives for better integration and participation in society.

169461.explanation: Farmers should not be allowed to build housing willy-nilly on their land. D66 wants to stimulate the creation of some new hamlets provided they fit into the landscape and do not have major infrastructural consequences for the surroundings.

169462.explanation: Not only for sports clubs but also for associations in art and culture.

169463.explanation: The municipality must enable every child to obtain a swimming diploma. There are better ways to achieve this than burdening schools with it. Many children already have a swimming diploma before school swimming, and school swimming replaces school gym.

169464.explanation: There is a multi-year maintenance plan that is being carried out.

169465.explanation: The municipality has a stimulating role.

169467.explanation: Allowing a coffeeshop fits a realistic and liberal policy. It ensures regulation, reduces nuisance, and takes soft drugs out of illegality. With clear rules, this can be safely and responsibly integrated.

169468.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on privacy. D66 prefers visible people in the neighborhood and prevention over permanent control.

169470.explanation: It must be easier for people with lower incomes to rent a home.

169471.explanation: Allowing permanent residence is a liberal and practical choice. It helps alleviate the housing shortage and aligns with changing tourist use. The homes must meet safety and quality-of-life requirements.

169472.explanation: Building only within village boundaries is too limited. We first look at whether infill is possible. Careful expansion where possible. Always with protection of landscape/nature and cultural-historical values.

169473.explanation: Not everyone on social assistance can do community work due to disabilities, care needs, or other personal circumstances. D66 chooses tailored solutions and guidance toward participation instead of an obligation that further pressures people.

169474.explanation: D66 wants to discourage car use in the city center by ensuring better accessibility through good cycling and walking paths.

169476.explanation: Provided safety, accessibility, and livability are not compromised.

169477.explanation: It must be someone knowledgeable, willing to get to know the municipality quickly, and seeking connection with residents.

169478.explanation: D66 strongly supports the so-called citizens' assembly, in which a randomly selected group of residents considers a municipal issue and has the opportunity to acquire knowledge about it. This to issue a weighty advisory recommendation to the council.

169480.explanation: Artificial intelligence is a useful tool, but nothing beats human contact. Behind a simple question can lie a deeper background that only surfaces by probing further, listening to a reaction, or if possible, observing body language.

169481.explanation: D66 supports renovation and adjustments within the framework of the ARBO (occupational health and safety) legislation. The theater must be suitable for small and medium-sized productions and appropriate for the size of Hardenberg. It should also be an incubator where municipal talent can develop.

169482.explanation: The municipality must ensure that residents aged 12 to 18 have limited access to a sport or cultural activity by providing vouchers.

169483.explanation: In the context of preventive health, we should challenge our residents to move more. We support initiatives to use exercise equipment.

169484.explanation: The survival of a school depends on a minimum number of students. The municipality must ensure that the distribution of public schools is such that they are accessible to everyone.

169485.explanation: Extra language support must be available for every child who needs it. Finances should not be an obstacle. The municipality must provide financial assistance where necessary. Good language skills are an investment in people's future.

169486.explanation: For reasons of accessibility, safety, and enforcement thereof, the municipality must grant permission.

169488.explanation: Where necessary, money should be spent on nature restoration but can also be used for restoring/building cycling and walking paths and other public facilities used by our residents and tourists.

169490.explanation: The municipality must fulfill its statutory duties.

169491.explanation: People should not remove tiles for financial gain but because they want a better and healthier living environment.

169492.explanation: Disposing of waste at home or at the recycling center costs money. It must remain affordable for both the municipality and residents.

169493.explanation: Provided the new park replaces an older, no longer used one and is comparable in size and staffing to the vacation park.

169494.explanation: This is not a priority for D66, also considering the recreational opportunities offered by the Hardenberg area.

169495.explanation: Collecting residual waste every six weeks increases the necessity to better separate our waste. Changing the collection frequency for one type of waste affects the separation of other types of waste.

169497.explanation: Control should be enforced around schools. However, displacement of dealing may be a consequence of control. D66 therefore wants to focus on detecting drug dealers by police and good education about the consequences of drug use.

169499.explanation: The municipality should not spend money on awareness campaigns already provided nationally. D66 focuses on education in digital developments through, for example, libraries.

169502.explanation: Social rental homes may only be sold if at least 30% remains available for social rent.

169503.explanation: Interns should receive an internship allowance equal to allowances for comparable internships. More importantly, interns should receive appropriate guidance and work that matches the development of their education.

169505.explanation: D66 does not see this as a municipal responsibility. However, the municipality could stimulate private initiatives.

169506.explanation: D66 does not want to attach a quota to this. The municipality must literally and figuratively remove obstacles for people who have difficulty finding work. Suitable employment and acceptance in the workplace are important. The municipality could serve as a role model in this.

169507.explanation: D66 wants to first focus on reducing car traffic to see what effect this has on traffic flow. Additionally, before making plans for a ring road, we want to have a clear vision of where we want Hardenberg to go spatially and economically in the future.

169509.explanation: D66 supports a traffic-free shopping center, so except for deliveries, it should be accessible only to pedestrians.

169510.explanation: There should be good facilities for pedestrians and cyclists, including a free covered bicycle parking.

169511.explanation: D66 wants to discourage car use in the city center. More expensive parking could help with this. Parking facilities must, however, be proportional to parking costs.

169512.explanation: Crosswalks near schools are often unsafe due to the behavior of parents who bring their children to school and prefer to park directly in front of the door. D66 prefers to invest in safe cycling routes, so that more children learn to safely participate in traffic by bike.

169513.explanation: Youth should have multiple opportunities to meet each other. This does not necessarily have to be a youth center. Survey young people themselves about what they need and how they want to organize it, and support them in implementation.

169514.explanation: D66 prefers introducing primary school children to municipal work and the democratic process through playful means. A children's mayor should not be a wish of the council but should come from the children themselves.

169516.explanation: Education often already offers this itself, and there are possibilities through so-called buddy projects. The municipality can only step in when finances are insufficient and the child risks being disadvantaged.

169452.explanation: Cooperation with other municipalities actually provides more room to maneuver within our own municipality.

169454.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be enabled to decide for themselves when they want to open their stores.

169456.explanation: If a distribution center wants to establish itself in the municipality of Hardenberg, it should be assessed at the time of application whether establishment is possible under the current situation. Do not immediately close the door.

169457.explanation: Asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands should be sent back immediately.

169458.explanation: No, there should also be a waiting list for them. We must also be able to serve our own residents who have been on the waiting list for a long time.

169459.explanation: Hardenberg has had a refugee center for many years within its borders. Now other municipalities are up.

169461.explanation: No, this is to prevent uncontrolled proliferation.

169462.explanation: Every child should have the opportunity to participate in sports. Parents who cannot afford this should be supported by the municipality.

169463.explanation: School swimming is necessary in our multicultural municipality. However, all children should then receive swimming lessons, also those from different backgrounds.

169464.explanation: School buildings must be well maintained.

169465.explanation: Dialect was previously considered somewhat unsocial, but our regional language must be preserved.

169466.explanation: More enforcement officers must be appointed. This will increase the sense of safety among our citizens. However, they must be visible and not just sitting in cars as often happens now.

169467.explanation: Agreed. The number of coffeeshops should not be expanded.

169468.explanation: For the safety of our residents and visitors, this is absolutely necessary.

169470.explanation: As many new social rental homes as possible must be built in the municipality of Hardenberg to quickly reduce waiting lists.

169471.explanation: No. If this happens, there is no longer any insight into who lives where. This can encourage crime.

169472.explanation: Where building is possible and allowed, it should also be possible.

169473.explanation: A counter-performance for receiving benefits is even regulated by law.

169474.explanation: Hardenberg must remain easily accessible by car. This is also important for our tourists.

169476.explanation: The tasks of the municipality are governmental tasks.

169477.explanation: It does not matter where someone has lived but the quality. An alderman from Hardenberg may have fewer qualities and therefore must step down.

169478.explanation: No. Residents may be asked for advice, but the final decision lies with the highest body in the municipality, the municipal council.

169480.explanation: No. There must be and remain personal contact.

169481.explanation: First look at renovation. If not possible, then new construction. This also strongly depends on the costs.

169482.explanation: The circumstances of the resident must also be considered. An 18-year-old can already have a good income. So look purely at the individual.

169483.explanation: A park on a square is not a fitness center.

169484.explanation: This depends strongly on the number of students. Schools cannot be kept open for a limited number of students, from a cost perspective.

169485.explanation: Children with a language delay must be supported to bring their language skills up to standard.

169486.explanation: This concerns small terraces (4 / 5 chairs). More is allowed with permission from the municipality.

169488.explanation: Tourist tax must benefit recreational facilities. It should not be a source of income for the municipality.

169490.explanation: We believe this is not a task for the municipality, but for the labor inspectorate.

169491.explanation: If someone wants greenery in their garden, only the option to have tiles collected free of charge should be provided.

169492.explanation: This can prevent illegal dumping. Although this is limited by a card system and also by a weight limit.

169493.explanation: Large holiday parks within the municipality of Hardenberg do not benefit our current recreational businesses. As a municipality, you should therefore not want this.

169494.explanation: SGH is a strong supporter of this. Families who cannot go on holiday abroad or similar can still have an enjoyable day with their children.

169495.explanation: Residual waste should be collected every 4 weeks and there must be a separate facility for diapers. This can also be arranged via ROVA in Zwolle, so why not in the municipality of Hardenberg?

169497.explanation: This can potentially map drug nuisance and other substance use more quickly, allowing necessary actions to follow.

169499.explanation: This information campaign should also be specifically aimed at the older residents of our municipality.

169502.explanation: Social rental housing must remain available for this purpose. Otherwise, it is like trying to mop up water while the tap remains open regarding the number of rental homes.

169503.explanation: There are many companies that do not pay any internship allowance at all; in that case, this amount is certainly reasonable.

169505.explanation: A pet is a great comfort for many single residents. When a low-income resident cannot afford the veterinarian, the municipality should step in. This helps prevent loneliness to a large extent.

169506.explanation: A staff number of 25 is too high. The number of new employees who have difficulty finding a job should be assessed to see how many can actually be placed. That number could be higher or lower.

169507.explanation: Traffic needs to flow better and a new ring road will definitely be necessary. For example, the Twenteweg regularly comes to a standstill with events held in the Event Hall. Ambulances and others are then also stuck. Also, freight traffic is getting heavier and more frequent.

169509.explanation: Certainly at certain times. For example, Monday mornings, shopping evenings, Saturdays, and during activities in the city.

169510.explanation: An excellent initiative, including places in such a parking facility to recharge electric bicycles.

169511.explanation: We must ensure parking remains affordable so that the city center is not ignored and people do not shop elsewhere because parking costs become too high. This is bad for our retailers.

169512.explanation: Crossings near schools should and can thereby become safer.

169513.explanation: This should become a meeting place to foster respect for those who are different.

169514.explanation: We do not consider a child mayor necessary.

169516.explanation: No, parents must be able to expect that their child can participate, like all children, and that this does not incur extra costs for the parents.

169452.explanation: Alone you go faster, together you go further. It costs a lot if everyone has to reinvent the wheel. However, there are partnerships that cost a lot of money, where mainly external people who are hired benefit more. We need to look critically at this. A collaboration must deliver results.

169454.explanation: We find it strange that the municipality decides whether a shop may open or not. This should be up to the entrepreneur.

169456.explanation: If we want to continue growing as a municipality, we must keep stimulating economic development.

169457.explanation: Anyone who is not allowed to stay in the Netherlands should not be accommodated in any municipality. Certainly not in Hardenberg. In the past ten years, the municipality of Hardenberg has already had the largest asylum center in Overijssel and has accommodated almost half of the refugees in the IJsselland region.

169458.explanation: Every resident of the municipality of Hardenberg deserves an equal chance in the housing market.

169459.explanation: In the past ten years, the municipality of Hardenberg has had the largest asylum center in Overijssel and has accommodated almost half of the refugees in the IJsselland region. We have more than fulfilled our responsibility. It is now up to other municipalities. No dispersal law means no asylum center.

169461.explanation: The agricultural sector is undergoing change and there is a housing crisis. Under fair and clear conditions, farmers should be able to develop on their own land. The rural feeling must not be lost. Therefore, we must look critically at the scale of the development.

169462.explanation: This target group often has to make worse choices regarding nutrition. Combined with too little exercise, this is a toxic mix that puts and keeps people at a disadvantage. We must be there for this group so that they are not left behind.

169463.explanation: We want school swimming to return. Every student in group 8 should have at least swimming diploma A.

169464.explanation: Some school buildings are still perfectly up-to-date. Not all buildings need an upgrade. The buildings that do need renovation will be renovated. In villages, we expand existing schools into educational institutions where childcare, education and youth care work together.

169465.explanation: Dutch is our official language; this is not a task for a school.

169466.explanation: Safety is one of DOEN'22's priorities. More police presence fits this. Furthermore, we think BOAs should also be equipped with defensive tools, such as handcuffs and a baton.

169467.explanation: Coffeeshops always bring crime on the back end. In Dedemsvaart, the only coffeeshop in our municipality also attracts many people from outside our municipality. We see this as a negative development. If we want to encourage healthy living, we must discourage drug sales.

169468.explanation: As DOEN'22, we consider safety very important. Nowadays everyone carries a phone with a camera. To more easily trace criminals or violent people, cameras are a good tool. Moreover, cameras also work preventively.

169470.explanation: Our municipality is currently below the national average. One solution to the housing crisis is building more social rental homes.

169471.explanation: Desperation breaks the law. In times of crisis, we will have to make decisions that do not always feel just. People who lived permanently in a holiday home were in violation. Not in every location is this possible, but for example, at park Moscou in Bergentheim, this can be a solution.

169472.explanation: We need more housing, so it is unwise to exclude such matters outright.

169473.explanation: This also helps people feel valued and reintegrate into paid work.

169474.explanation: Many visitors of our shopping center come from outside the city of Hardenberg itself.

169476.explanation: This is primarily a municipal responsibility. The public space must be well maintained by the municipality. This can still be improved significantly, especially in rural areas. We want to support residents who want to keep their own environment tidy.

169477.explanation: The municipality currently has 4 aldermen. Only 1 has lived here for 10 years. 2 of the 4 do not live in our municipality. As an alderman, you must be part of the community. You need to know the people and understand the mentality and DNA. This is currently lacking and, in our opinion, must change.

169478.explanation: We prefer to rely on specialists.

169480.explanation: People actually have more need for real contact. The municipality's service must be more customer-friendly and better.

169481.explanation: For us, new does not necessarily mean new construction. Renovation is also a serious option. It is too early to say that new construction must take place. It is also too early to say it should not happen. Therefore, after thorough research, we must make a wise decision.

169482.explanation: Parents of children, or children with sufficient funds, can easily pay for this themselves.

169483.explanation: People want to exercise in a gym, not in a square. In practice, you see that equipment currently in public spaces is hardly or not used at all.

169484.explanation: In villages, we also expand existing schools into educational institutions where childcare, education, and youth care work together.

169485.explanation: Often these are parents with little money. If we want to give everyone a fair chance, we must be there especially for this target group.

169486.explanation: This causes clutter in public spaces. It is important for the municipality to keep this under control. However, we do think the municipality should have a positive attitude towards entrepreneurs who want to place a terrace. Entrepreneurs may of course decide for themselves on their own property.

169488.explanation: The tourist tax should be used to keep the living environment attractive. This can include nature but also maintenance of public spaces. Small things like cleaning place name signs and signage just before the tourist season, for example.

169490.explanation: One of the main causes of the housing shortage is labor migration. Some labor migrants are exploited. This should receive more attention.

169491.explanation: People should be free to design their own gardens.

169492.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping of waste. Especially now that residual waste containers are collected less frequently.

169493.explanation: An entrepreneur must be able to continue doing business, including in recreation. However, we must critically assess whether the development fits the area.

169494.explanation: This would be a nice addition to the city center.

169495.explanation: As DOEN'22, we opposed reducing the frequency of residual waste collection. We would like this measure reversed and at least want the PMD (Plastic, Metal, and Drink cartons) to be collected more frequently.

169497.explanation: Drugs are an increasing problem, also in our municipality. We must tackle this very firmly.

169499.explanation: Criminals are becoming increasingly digital. We must be aware of this. More attention should also be given to combating digital bullying.

169502.explanation: Although you have to be honest that you cannot always prevent this...

169503.explanation: This is already a great amount for a student.

169505.explanation: This is not a municipal responsibility, however painful that may be.

169506.explanation: With the goal of a permanent contract in the long term.

169507.explanation: It is important that Hardenberg is and remains easily accessible. Now and in the future.

169509.explanation: Near-accidents with electric bikes and especially fatbikes happen regularly in the shopping center. We will only implement a bike ban after a good solution for bike parking has been found. There must also be a solution first for people with reduced mobility.

169510.explanation: Near-accidents with electric bikes and especially fatbikes happen regularly in the shopping center. We want to introduce a bike ban after a good solution for bike parking has been found. A solution must also first exist for people with reduced mobility.

169511.explanation: This is unnecessary and unfriendly to customers.

169512.explanation: Safety above all, especially for our children.

169513.explanation: There is too little to do in our municipality, especially for young people under 18.

169514.explanation: It is good to involve our youth early on in public governance.

169516.explanation: Not everyone can afford this. Parents who can pay themselves should of course do so.

169452.explanation: Scaling up must not come at the expense of identity and local democratic control. Cooperation can be entered into provided the above points are not compromised.

169454.explanation: Sunday is a day of rest given by God. The SGP is fundamentally against shops being open on this day to protect Sunday rest.

169456.explanation: Precious agricultural land must not be sacrificed to large-scale 'graying' of the landscape. Moreover, traffic pressure and density increase, which negatively affect our municipal infrastructure and traffic safety, among other things.

169457.explanation: Enforcement of order and rules is essential. Those not allowed to stay must return.

169458.explanation: Local residents (starters/seniors) must not be disadvantaged by priority arrangements for status holders.

169459.explanation: Shelter must be balanced with the local community's capacity. Large-scale, permanent shelter disrupts peace and safety.

169461.explanation: The SGP is careful with our precious farmland; this is the basis for our food production and must be protected against irreversible building. However, the SGP supports optimal use of existing property, such as building care homes and temporary housing for young people.

169462.explanation: The most vulnerable in society (such as children from low-income families) must be supported to participate.

169463.explanation: Safety in and around water is important for children in our water-rich municipality. School swimming is an excellent way to protect children from the potential dangers of water.

169464.explanation: The municipality is responsible for adequate educational housing as a prerequisite for development.

169465.explanation: The regional language is part of the local identity. The SGP wants to reinforce local traditions and the sense of community.

169466.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) are necessary for safety in cities, villages, and rural areas. Protecting residents is a core task. The SGP supports increasing the number of BOAs focused on safety.

169467.explanation: The SGP wants to combat undermining crime and addiction. Enforcement of Christian norms and safety is paramount.

169468.explanation: Camera surveillance can be used as a tool to ensure safety at locations where necessary, to protect residents and entrepreneurs.

169470.explanation: Building should meet the needs of local residents, with sufficient social rental housing. The SGP supports aiming for 30% social rental in new construction.

169471.explanation: Recreational locations should retain their character. Permanent residence is only seen as a temporary 'emergency solution' for housing shortages.

169472.explanation: The SGP wants building primarily within village centers (infill rather than expansion) to keep young and old in their villages. When infill offers too few possibilities, limited expansion ('just an extra street') is acceptable, but not entire new neighborhoods.

169473.explanation: Benefits are a temporary safety net; where possible, a social return is appropriate.

169474.explanation: Good accessibility to vital facilities and shops is necessary for the local economy.

169476.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs must be given space to address matters in a better and cheaper way themselves. Neighborliness and personal responsibility are central.

169477.explanation: Disagree. However, we do prefer that an alderman, as a rule, lives or will come to live in the municipality they govern.

169478.explanation: Authority comes from God, not only from the people. The SGP values representative democracy and the primacy of the council.

169480.explanation: AI offers opportunities as a tool for greater efficiency, provided it does not replace human responsibility. The possibility to speak to someone in person during municipal office hours must be maintained.

169481.explanation: The SGP stands for a frugal government that first focuses on the preservation and renovation of existing heritage and buildings.

169482.explanation: Subsidies should target the most vulnerable. The SGP chooses a sober and efficient financial policy.

169483.explanation: Not a primary government task. The SGP does encourage a healthy living environment, but through personal responsibility.

169484.explanation: Preserving schools in every village is essential for the livability of our strong centers and villages.

169485.explanation: Combating educational disadvantages is an important municipal responsibility because it contributes to equal opportunities and a strong future for every child.

169486.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be given room. The municipality should take a facilitating role and create conditions for entrepreneurship.

169488.explanation: Nature restoration is important to the SGP. Stewardship means that revenues from the environment (tourism) are invested in its management (nature). This way, we keep our nature vital and everyone can continue to enjoy it.

169490.explanation: Exploitation is unacceptable. The municipality must oversee good housing and registration (night register).

169491.explanation: The SGP supports encouraging the replacement of paving stones with greenery. This fits within 'Operation Stone Break' and climate adaptation and helps reduce water nuisance and combat heat stress.

169492.explanation: Stewardship demands careful management of resources. The polluter pays to encourage waste separation.

169493.explanation: The economy must serve the community and the landscape; large-scale parks can harm the peace and identity of our municipality.

169494.explanation: Government money is spent frugally on core tasks (safety, care). Luxuries like a city beach have no priority.

169495.explanation: Less frequent collection encourages separation of resources, which aligns with stewardship. At the same time, there must be sufficient opportunities to separate (residual) waste in between so that (residual) waste does not become litter. This prevents pollution and environmental damage.

169497.explanation: Protecting youth against drugs and crime is an absolute priority.

169499.explanation: The municipality has the task of protecting residents. Preventing (online) crime is part of caring for safety.

169502.explanation: The SGP believes this can be a way to promote circulation in the housing market, provided it remains balanced with the social needs of our municipality.

169503.explanation: The municipality must be an attractive and reliable employer that values young talent.

169505.explanation: Not a core municipal task. The SGP focuses on mercy for people in need and support for the vulnerable.

169506.explanation: The municipality sets a good example by offering jobs to people with a distance to the labor market.

169507.explanation: Traffic safety is crucial; the SGP wants to guide traffic flows so that everyone can participate safely. The focus is on flow around the city.

169509.explanation: SGP is against a general bicycle ban in the city center, but supports research into a ban on fat bikes. Safety in busy areas comes first and nuisances are addressed. SGP Hardenberg also advocates investing in safe bike paths and better cycling infrastructure.

169510.explanation: Encouraging cycling contributes to livability and air quality in the city center.

169511.explanation: The tax burden on residents and entrepreneurs must remain limited to keep the local economy vital.

169512.explanation: Traffic safety around schools has the highest priority ('every life counts').

169513.explanation: For livability and social cohesion, meeting places in the centers are valuable.

169514.explanation: The SGP opts for down-to-earth politics without unnecessary ceremonial functions.

169516.explanation: The government is a safety net for those who really need it. The focus is on the efficient use of resources for intensive care.

169452.explanation: Collaboration can sometimes yield more, especially with matters that overlap, for example a road that runs through two municipalities.

169454.explanation: We live in a 24-hour economy, so that fits in nicely. Entrepreneurs should have the choice whether they want to participate or not.

169456.explanation: Distribution centers are good for employment, so they are definitely allowed. Of course, there must be careful consideration of where they are located.

169457.explanation: Hardenberg is for our own people who, partly because of all those refugees, cannot get a house. Also, security cannot be guaranteed because of them. So no, no more asylum seeker shelters in the municipality.

169458.explanation: Status holders should simply be placed at the bottom of the list and then selected by lottery so that everyone has an equal chance. Our own children also want to live independently.

169459.explanation: We have had them for more than 10 years (too long) in the municipality. That's enough, no more. Never.

169461.explanation: The municipality must ensure that there is no uncontrolled growth of houses. But it should certainly be possible. Caregiver homes also fall under this.

169462.explanation: To pay for a sports club, we already have the Social Cultural Fund which parents can apply for.

169463.explanation: We have a lot of water in the municipality, so it should be standard that every child can swim. At least the basic techniques.

169464.explanation: Every child must be able to get to school safely and sit there safely. That is why schools must be adapted to current standards.

169465.explanation: First give attention to basic Dutch, math, and school swimming.

169466.explanation: Partly because there is little police presence, there is a feeling of insecurity. This can partly be solved by having more enforcement officers on the streets! But in the end, more police will be needed!

169467.explanation: By centralizing it, it is also easier to control. Otherwise, it becomes uncontrolled growth.

169468.explanation: Safety comes first. Camera footage must be deleted if nothing has happened.

169470.explanation: There are too few social rental homes, so it is important for young and old that more are built as soon as possible.

169471.explanation: Look at the percentage of homes allowed per park. Each park must certainly still have places for tourists.

169472.explanation: Villages and towns should be able to grow, but this may not come at the cost of nature.

169473.explanation: This way they stay among people and retain work experience. That may prove advantageous in the future.

169474.explanation: For people with mobility issues, but also for people from the villages, Hardenberg must remain easily accessible. Since public transport is also lacking, and cycling with three shopping bags is not ideal.

169476.explanation: A local entrepreneur who can do it better and cheaper should definitely be engaged. That is also good for the local economy.

169477.explanation: Some connection with the municipality is at least the minimum a councilor should have. Someone who lives here or grew up here understands our opinions best.

169478.explanation: Residents of the municipality should have more say in important decisions that affect them. The municipal council must genuinely act on the results of that group.

169480.explanation: Artificial intelligence is not yet completely reliable. People want personal contact, not with a computer.

169481.explanation: The location of the current theater is a good spot and can therefore be renovated. Visitors are also close to the center, which is good for entrepreneurs.

169482.explanation: Parents who cannot afford it for their children can apply to the social cultural fund. This can be used to pay for it.

169483.explanation: It is better to exercise at a gym. In public places without supervision, the risk of injury is too high.

169484.explanation: Every village should have a public school. At this school, you receive education about different religions, allowing you to choose yourself instead of everything being focused on one particular religion.

169485.explanation: Parents who are partly to blame themselves must pay. If other factors are involved, they must pay part themselves.

169486.explanation: In consultation with the municipality to avoid uncontrolled growth in impossible places or on parking lots and the like.

169488.explanation: The money must be used where it is needed. That can be nature but also other things.

169490.explanation: No one may be exploited regardless of who you are, where you come from, or what color you have! Strong action must definitely be taken against this.

169491.explanation: The municipality already has this at the moment. We definitely support this subsidy!

169492.explanation: Every address in the municipality should be able to dispose of bulky waste for free at least once a year. With a maximum of 500 kg.

169493.explanation: A large holiday park brings a lot of employment and will also mean extra turnover for local entrepreneurs. However, any damage to nature and the environment must be carefully considered.

169494.explanation: No matter how beautiful it can be, we already have the Oldemeijer and the Kotermeerstal. We can better spend the money on other important things first.

169495.explanation: As long as the underground container system is not fully rolled out, it should return to four weeks. The cost of individual bags must never be more expensive than the container.

169497.explanation: A lot of drugs are dealt near schools, which is of course unacceptable, so the more control, the better. Time must be allocated for this.

169499.explanation: The municipality can certainly do something about this, but a real campaign is the responsibility of the national government. But every little bit helps!

169502.explanation: We already lack a lot of rental homes and selling them will ensure that the shortage remains.

169503.explanation: The compensation in the municipality of Hardenberg is at the national average, so it's fine as it is.

169505.explanation: Everyone must pay for veterinary costs themselves; it is also a personal choice to have a pet, and you can be aware that there may be medical expenses involved. There is a foundation that can potentially help.

169506.explanation: The municipality should only hire people who are needed, not a fixed number. Having a certain percentage of people who have difficulty finding a job might be possible, but not a fixed number.

169507.explanation: You can already have a nice ride around Hardenberg. First invest the money in other projects such as safe intersections in other parts of the municipality.

169509.explanation: Definitely a bicycle ban, but not only in the Hardenberg shopping center, also for example the Marktstraat in Dedemsvaart.

169510.explanation: If you want fewer cars, free bicycle parking is a good solution. Put solar panels on the roofs and we kill two birds with one stone. This also applies to other centers in our municipality.

169511.explanation: Parking fees may increase with inflation, but not beyond that. The maintenance of parking places is also becoming more expensive.

169512.explanation: At every school, a child must be able to arrive and leave safely. This requires a lot of attention. Safety first.

169513.explanation: There is already so little for the youth to do. It is good to consult with them about what needs to be organized. Also ensure a good supervisor to do this together with the youth!

169514.explanation: We are not Madurodam! Let children play and roughhouse freely. However, once or twice a year we can sit at a table with a group of children so they can also have their voice heard by the municipality!

169516.explanation: Parents with children who need light youth care due to circumstances or illness do not have to pay for it.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a bypass (ring road) around the town of Hardenberg.
statements.moreInfo: A ring road is a road around a city that directs through-traffic away from the center and residential areas.
statements.pro: A ring road makes Hardenberg more accessible. You can more easily get from one side of town to the other. Heavy traffic will use the ring road more often and less often go through the center and residential areas. That makes traffic safer.
statements.con: Building a bypass costs a lot of money. The municipality could spend the money better on other things. Constructing a bypass comes at the expense of nature and the living environment.

statements.title: Shops should always be allowed to open on Sundays.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities decide for themselves whether shops are open or closed on Sundays. In the municipality of Hardenberg there are currently 10 shopping Sundays per year. Supermarkets may be open every Sunday.
statements.pro: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves whether to open their shops on Sundays. Residents should be able to choose which day they want to shop.
statements.con: Sunday rest is important for many residents with a Christian background. There must remain a good balance between work and private life, also for entrepreneurs.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
statements.moreInfo: Building on land designated for agriculture and horticulture is not automatically allowed. If a farmer wants to build a house on his land, he can ask the municipality to give that land a residential zoning.
statements.pro: There are too few houses. All types of housing are needed. Caregiver homes are needed for people who provide or receive care. These can be built on released agricultural land.
statements.con: There are already regulations for this. The municipality should steer the construction of new homes to prevent uncontrolled growth.

statements.title: Residents and local entrepreneurs should be able to take over municipal tasks if they can do them better and cheaper, for example maintaining a green strip.
statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can give residents the right to challenge the municipality: the challenge right. A resident who thinks they can do municipal tasks better or more cheaply may take over those tasks. For example, maintaining public green spaces or organizing activities in the community center.
statements.pro: It is always good for the municipality if things are done better and cheaper. Then there is money left for other matters. By doing it themselves, residents have more influence on their neighborhood.
statements.con: The municipality must remain responsible for tasks such as maintaining public green spaces. Residents can help voluntarily. They should not receive payment for that.

statements.title: The municipality should encourage attention to the local dialect in all primary schools.
statements.moreInfo: In Hardenberg the Sallands dialect is spoken, a regional language that belongs to the Low Saxon language group. The municipality cannot oblige schools to pay attention to the dialect. The municipality can, however, ask schools to give more attention to the dialect.
statements.pro: It is important that we pass the regional language on to children and young people. Attention to the dialect is a good addition to the curriculum. The municipality should promote the use of the regional language.
statements.con: The municipality should not interfere with primary school curricula. Dutch is our language of instruction. Schools should primarily focus on basic subjects such as Dutch and math.

statements.title: The municipality should run an information campaign to help residents protect themselves against online dangers, such as digital scams.
statements.moreInfo: Each year 2.3 million Dutch people fall victim to digital crime. That is crime via computer, phone or tablet, for example digital threats and intimidation or online scams and fraud.
statements.pro: It is important that residents protect themselves better against digital scammers. With an information campaign the municipality can explain how they can do that.
statements.con: An information campaign to help residents against online risks is not a task for the municipality. That should be done by the national government.

statements.title: The municipality should pay veterinary costs for low-income people.
statements.moreInfo: A visit to the veterinarian has become increasingly expensive in recent years. Not all pet owners can afford these costs.
statements.pro: Having a pet is good for health and, for example, also helps against loneliness.
statements.con: Anyone who takes a pet must pay for it themselves. The municipality is not responsible for that.

statements.title: Social rental homes may be sold after the tenants move out.
statements.moreInfo: For a social rental home you pay lower rent. They are usually owned by a housing corporation. Only people with a lower income can get such a home. Many people want such a home. The waiting list is long.
statements.pro: By selling social rental homes the housing corporation gets more money. With that money they can build more homes.
statements.con: There are already too few social rental homes in Hardenberg. The existing social housing should be preserved.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' centre (azc) should remain in the municipality of Hardenberg.
statements.moreInfo: Since 2016 the municipality of Hardenberg has housed 700 asylum seekers in the asylum seekers’ centre (azc) on Jachthuisweg. This is the largest asylum accommodation in Overijssel. In March 2026 this centre will close. There is a large shortage of accommodation places for asylum seekers throughout the Netherlands.
statements.pro: The municipality of Hardenberg must help people who have fled from war and violence.
statements.con: In the past 10 years the municipality of Hardenberg hosted the largest asylum seeker center in Overijssel. It is now up to other municipalities to accommodate asylum seekers.

statements.title: Hospitality businesses should be allowed to open small outdoor terraces without asking the municipality for permission.
statements.moreInfo: Cafés and restaurants must apply for a permit if they want to open a terrace. The municipality of Hardenberg then checks whether they meet all the rules, for example whether there is enough space on the pavement for pedestrians.
statements.pro: Small terraces involve few chairs and tables. That causes hardly any nuisance. Entrepreneurs should be given space to do business.
statements.con: We must prevent the streets from becoming chaotic. A terrace must never block passage for emergency services. Therefore the municipality must always check whether a terrace is safe at a given location.

statements.title: There should be a bicycle ban in the shopping center of the town of Hardenberg.
statements.moreInfo: Since the beginning of last year there has been the Green Bike Route in the center of Hardenberg. This route is meant to guide cyclists along the shopping area to improve safety. Nevertheless, cyclists continue to ride through the shopping area.
statements.pro: Accidents and near-accidents often happen in the shopping center. A bicycle ban makes the shopping area safer for shoppers. In recent years the shopping area has become busier. There is no longer room for cyclists.
statements.con: The municipality must provide good bicycle parking at the edges of the center. Only after that can a bike ban be introduced. Customers should be able to easily reach shops and hospitality venues. A bike ban in the shopping area reduces accessibility.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
statements.moreInfo: Holiday homes are intended for tourists. They are often located in a holiday park or on a campsite. In principle it is forbidden in the Netherlands to use holiday homes as a permanent address. Due to the housing shortage this still happens: about 60,000 people live in a holiday home. The government wants to allow permanent living in holiday homes. The minister is asking municipalities to already permit permanent living in holiday homes.
statements.pro: In times of housing shortage it is a solution for many people to live permanently in a holiday home.
statements.con: Holiday homes are built for tourism. They are not suitable as a permanent address.

statements.title: The municipality should have simple questions answered by artificial intelligence, for example via a chatbot.
statements.moreInfo: Artificial intelligence means that machines can imitate human abilities, such as reasoning and planning. A chatbot is a computer program that can answer questions, for example questions from residents to the municipality.
statements.pro: The municipality must use new technologies. A chatbot is cheaper than having an employee answer questions.
statements.con: People need personal contact. They do not want a computer to answer their questions. Artificial intelligence is still too unreliable. The chance that residents will receive incorrect answers from a chatbot is too great.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.
statements.moreInfo: A coffeeshop is a shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed). The municipality of Hardenberg currently has 1 coffeeshop in Dedemsvaart.
statements.pro: Residents should be able to buy soft drugs in a safe place. If an entrepreneur follows the rules, there is no reason for the municipality to block a coffeeshop.
